datasets.description: Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding benchmark spanning six broad disciplines and 30 subjects. Tests college-level visual reasoning over diagrams, charts, plots, chemical structures, etc.
datasets.specialty: Strong filter — frontier MLLMs still leave ~30 pp headroom on the harder subjects.

datasets.description: Open dataset of 5.85B CLIP-filtered image-text pairs scraped from Common Crawl. The canonical open-source pretraining set for Stable Diffusion-class models.
datasets.specialty: Quality is heterogeneous; consider the higher-aesthetic subsets for fine-tuning.

datasets.description: Visual question answering over real-world charts, mixing human-authored and templated questions. Requires reading axes, comparing values, and doing arithmetic on chart-derived numbers.
datasets.specialty: Splits cleanly into 'augmented' (template) and 'human' subsets — most models overfit to the easier one.

datasets.description: 100 hours of multi-modal egocentric video shot in 45 home kitchens, with dense action, narration, and gaze annotations. Standard target for fine-grained action / anticipation benchmarks.
datasets.specialty: Hands-on-object density makes this the de-facto eval for embodied vision-language work.

datasets.description: 56-task embedding-quality benchmark covering retrieval, classification, clustering, reranking, STS, and more. The de-facto leaderboard for embedding models.
datasets.specialty: Watch for task leakage in newer embedding models — several frontier models trained on MTEB train splits.

datasets.description: 812 realistic web-browsing tasks on self-hosted clones of Shopify, GitLab, Reddit, and CMS. Evaluates end-to-end multi-step task completion by agents.
datasets.specialty: Single-host eval — pass-rate numbers are reproducible only with identical Docker tags.
